Package dk.tij.registermaschine.core.error

package dk.tij.registermaschine.core.error
Defines the exception hierarchy for the Registermaschine engine.

This package centralises error handling to ensure that failure in different stages of the machine's lifecycle-compilation, configuration, and execution are distinguishable and carry sufficient context (such as line numbers or opcodes).

Exception Categories:
  • Compilation Errors: Thrown when the source code violates lexical or syntactic rules (e.g., SyntaxErrorException).
  • Configuration Errors: Thrown when the .jxml instruction sets or hardware settings are malformed.
  • Runtime errors: Thrown during the execution loop, typically representing illegal state transitions or invalid register access.
Since:
1.0.0
Author:
TiJ
  • Exception Classes
    Class
    Description
    ClassInstantiationException
    Thrown when a class cannot be instantiated via reflection or other dynamic mechanisms.
    ConditionParseException
    Thrown when a condition expression cannot be parsed or is invalid.
    ConfigurationParseException
    Thrown when a configuration file or object cannot be parsed correctly.
    DefectPipelineException
    Thrown when an instruction pipeline is broken or cannot be executed properly due to internal errors.
    ExistingInstructionException
    Thrown when attempting to register an instruction that already exists in the instruction set.
    SyntaxErrorException
    Thrown when there is a syntax error in the source code.
    UnknownInstructionException
    Thrown when an instruction cannot be identified or is not registered in the current instruction set.
